description: Create src/claude_agent/structured_errors.py module file with module docstring explaining the purpose of structured error classification for intelligent recovery
steps: Step 1: Verify src/claude_agent/ directory exists
Step 2: Create structured_errors.py with module docstring
Step 3: Verify file is created with proper structure

description: Define ErrorType enum with RETRY, MANUAL, FATAL, TIMEOUT values in structured_errors.py
steps: Step 1: Import Enum from enum module
Step 2: Create ErrorType(str, Enum) with four values
Step 3: Verify enum values: ErrorType.RETRY, ErrorType.MANUAL, ErrorType.FATAL, ErrorType.TIMEOUT

description: Define ErrorCategory enum with NETWORK, AUTH, LOGIC, CONFIG, RESOURCE, SECURITY, VALIDATION values in structured_errors.py
steps: Step 1: Create ErrorCategory(str, Enum) with seven values
Step 2: Verify all categories exist: NETWORK, AUTH, LOGIC, CONFIG, RESOURCE, SECURITY, VALIDATION
Step 3: Verify string representation works correctly

description: Create StructuredError dataclass with type, category, message, recovery_hint, timestamp, and context fields
steps: Step 1: Import dataclass, field, datetime
Step 2: Create StructuredError dataclass with all required fields
Step 3: Verify recovery_hint defaults to None
Step 4: Verify context defaults to empty dict
Step 5: Verify timestamp is datetime type

description: Implement StructuredError.to_dict() method for JSON serialization
steps: Step 1: Add to_dict() method to StructuredError
Step 2: Serialize all fields including enum values as strings
Step 3: Convert timestamp to ISO format string
Step 4: Test serialization produces valid JSON-compatible dict

description: Implement StructuredError.from_dict() class method for deserialization
steps: Step 1: Add from_dict(data: dict) classmethod
Step 2: Parse enum values from strings
Step 3: Parse timestamp from ISO format
Step 4: Handle missing optional fields gracefully
Step 5: Test roundtrip: to_dict() -> from_dict() preserves data

description: Implement StructuredError.is_retryable() helper method returning True for RETRY type
steps: Step 1: Add is_retryable() method
Step 2: Return True if self.type == ErrorType.RETRY
Step 3: Return False for other error types
Step 4: Verify MANUAL, FATAL, TIMEOUT return False

description: Implement StructuredError.requires_human() helper method returning True for MANUAL type
steps: Step 1: Add requires_human() method
Step 2: Return True if self.type == ErrorType.MANUAL
Step 3: Return False for RETRY, FATAL, TIMEOUT types

description: Create error_security_block(command, reason) factory function returning StructuredError
steps: Step 1: Define function with command and reason parameters
Step 2: Return StructuredError with type=MANUAL, category=SECURITY
Step 3: Include config path hint in recovery_hint
Step 4: Include command in context dict

description: Create error_validation_failed(feature_index, reason) factory function returning StructuredError
steps: Step 1: Define function with feature_index and reason parameters
Step 2: Return StructuredError with type=MANUAL, category=VALIDATION
Step 3: Include feature index in context
Step 4: Provide actionable recovery hint

description: Create error_file_not_found(path) factory function returning StructuredError
steps: Step 1: Define function with path parameter
Step 2: Return StructuredError with type=MANUAL, category=RESOURCE
Step 3: Include file path in context
Step 4: Suggest checking path existence in recovery_hint

description: Create error_git_operation(operation, details) factory function returning StructuredError
steps: Step 1: Define function with operation and details parameters
Step 2: Return StructuredError with type=RETRY, category=RESOURCE
Step 3: Include operation name in context
Step 4: Suggest git status check in recovery_hint

description: Create error_test_failure(test_type, details) factory function for lint/type/test failures
steps: Step 1: Define function with test_type and details parameters
Step 2: Return StructuredError with type=MANUAL, category=LOGIC
Step 3: Include test type in context
Step 4: Suggest reviewing test output in recovery_hint

description: Create error_agent_timeout(agent_type, duration_seconds) factory function
steps: Step 1: Define function with agent_type and duration_seconds parameters
Step 2: Return StructuredError with type=TIMEOUT, category=RESOURCE
Step 3: Include agent type and duration in context
Step 4: Suggest increasing timeout or simplifying task in recovery_hint

description: Implement StructuredError.to_actionable_error() method for CLI display bridge
steps: Step 1: Import ActionableError from claude_agent.errors
Step 2: Map StructuredError message to ActionableError message
Step 3: Map recovery_hint to context field
Step 4: Map category to help_command based on error type
Step 5: Verify ActionableError output is human-readable

description: Create tests/test_structured_errors.py test file with test class structure
steps: Step 1: Create test file with pytest imports
Step 2: Add test classes for ErrorType, ErrorCategory, StructuredError
Step 3: Verify test file structure follows existing patterns

description: Write unit tests for ErrorType enum values and string representation
steps: Step 1: Test all four enum values exist
Step 2: Test string representation is lowercase
Step 3: Test enum comparison works correctly

description: Write unit tests for ErrorCategory enum values
steps: Step 1: Test all seven category values exist
Step 2: Test string representation matches expected format
Step 3: Test enum iteration covers all categories

description: Write unit tests for StructuredError dataclass creation and defaults
steps: Step 1: Test creation with required fields only
Step 2: Test default values for optional fields
Step 3: Test creation with all fields specified

description: Write unit tests for StructuredError serialization roundtrip
steps: Step 1: Create StructuredError with all fields
Step 2: Call to_dict() and verify JSON-serializable
Step 3: Call from_dict() and verify equality
Step 4: Test handling of missing optional fields

description: Write unit tests for StructuredError helper methods is_retryable() and requires_human()
steps: Step 1: Test is_retryable() returns True only for RETRY type
Step 2: Test requires_human() returns True only for MANUAL type
Step 3: Test both methods with all error types

description: Write unit tests for all six error factory functions
steps: Step 1: Test error_security_block returns correct type and category
Step 2: Test error_validation_failed includes feature_index in context
Step 3: Test error_file_not_found includes path in context
Step 4: Test error_git_operation returns RETRY type
Step 5: Test error_test_failure returns LOGIC category
Step 6: Test error_agent_timeout returns TIMEOUT type

description: Write unit tests for to_actionable_error() bridge method
steps: Step 1: Test message mapping is correct
Step 2: Test recovery_hint becomes context
Step 3: Test output is valid ActionableError instance

description: Create src/claude_agent/state.py module file for XDG state management
steps: Step 1: Create state.py with module docstring explaining XDG state separation
Step 2: Import required modules (os, pathlib, json, hashlib)
Step 3: Verify file is created with proper structure

description: Implement get_state_dir() function returning XDG-compliant state directory path
steps: Step 1: Check XDG_STATE_HOME environment variable
Step 2: Fall back to ~/.local/state if not set
Step 3: Return path with /claude-agent/ suffix
Step 4: Verify path is absolute

description: Implement get_project_hash(project_dir) function returning 12-char SHA256 hash
steps: Step 1: Convert project_dir to absolute path string
Step 2: Compute SHA256 hash of path
Step 3: Return first 12 characters of hexdigest
Step 4: Verify hash is stable across calls with same path

description: Implement get_workflow_dir(project_dir) function returning workflow state directory
steps: Step 1: Call get_state_dir() for base path
Step 2: Call get_project_hash() for project identifier
Step 3: Return path: {state_dir}/workflows/{hash}/
Step 4: Verify returned path structure is correct

description: Implement get_logs_dir() function returning XDG logs directory
steps: Step 1: Call get_state_dir() for base path
Step 2: Return path: {state_dir}/logs/
Step 3: Verify path is under XDG state directory

description: Implement ensure_state_dirs() function that creates directories with 0o700 permissions
steps: Step 1: Create state dir, workflows dir, and logs dir
Step 2: Set permissions to 0o700 (user-only access)
Step 3: Handle existing directories gracefully
Step 4: Verify directories are created with correct permissions

description: Create WorkflowState dataclass with all required fields
steps: Step 1: Define dataclass with id, project_dir, phase, started_at, updated_at fields
Step 2: Add features_completed, features_total, current_feature_index fields
Step 3: Add iteration_count, last_error, pause_reason, recovery_steps fields
Step 4: Set appropriate default values
Step 5: Verify all fields have correct types

description: Add phase validation to WorkflowState ensuring only valid phase values
steps: Step 1: Define VALID_PHASES = {'initializing', 'coding', 'validating', 'complete', 'paused'}
Step 2: Add __post_init__ validation for phase field
Step 3: Raise ValueError for invalid phase values
Step 4: Test with all valid and invalid phase values

description: Implement WorkflowState.to_dict() method for JSON serialization
steps: Step 1: Serialize all fields to JSON-compatible dict
Step 2: Convert datetime fields to ISO format strings
Step 3: Handle None values appropriately
Step 4: Verify output is JSON-serializable

description: Implement WorkflowState.from_dict() classmethod for deserialization
steps: Step 1: Parse datetime fields from ISO format
Step 2: Handle missing optional fields with defaults
Step 3: Validate phase value
Step 4: Test roundtrip serialization preserves data

description: Implement load_workflow_state(project_dir) function returning WorkflowState or None
steps: Step 1: Compute workflow directory for project
Step 2: Check for workflow-state.json file
Step 3: Return None if file doesn't exist
Step 4: Parse JSON and return WorkflowState.from_dict()
Step 5: Handle JSON parse errors gracefully (return None)

description: Implement save_workflow_state(state) function with atomic write
steps: Step 1: Compute workflow directory from state.project_dir
Step 2: Update state.updated_at to current time
Step 3: Write to temp file first
Step 4: Rename temp file to workflow-state.json (atomic)
Step 5: Verify file contains correct JSON

description: Implement clear_workflow_state(project_dir) function deleting state file
steps: Step 1: Compute workflow directory for project
Step 2: Delete workflow-state.json if exists
Step 3: Return True if file existed and was deleted
Step 4: Return True if file didn't exist (idempotent)
Step 5: Handle permission errors gracefully

description: Create tests/test_state.py test file with test class structure
steps: Step 1: Create test file with pytest imports
Step 2: Add test classes for path functions and WorkflowState
Step 3: Use tmp_path fixture for file system tests

description: Write unit tests for get_state_dir() function
steps: Step 1: Test returns path under ~/.local/state by default
Step 2: Test respects XDG_STATE_HOME environment variable
Step 3: Test path ends with /claude-agent/

description: Write unit tests for get_project_hash() stability and format
steps: Step 1: Test hash is exactly 12 characters
Step 2: Test hash is stable (same input = same output)
Step 3: Test different paths produce different hashes
Step 4: Test hash is hexadecimal

description: Write unit tests for WorkflowState dataclass creation and validation
steps: Step 1: Test creation with valid phase values
Step 2: Test ValueError raised for invalid phase
Step 3: Test default values are applied correctly

description: Write unit tests for WorkflowState serialization roundtrip
steps: Step 1: Create WorkflowState with all fields
Step 2: Serialize with to_dict()
Step 3: Deserialize with from_dict()
Step 4: Verify equality of all fields

description: Write unit tests for load_workflow_state() and save_workflow_state() functions
steps: Step 1: Test load returns None for non-existent file
Step 2: Test save creates file with correct content
Step 3: Test load returns correct WorkflowState after save
Step 4: Test save updates updated_at timestamp

description: Write unit tests for clear_workflow_state() function
steps: Step 1: Test returns True when file exists and is deleted
Step 2: Test returns True when file doesn't exist (idempotent)
Step 3: Verify file is actually removed from filesystem

description: Add last_error field integration to WorkflowState for StructuredError persistence
steps: Step 1: Ensure last_error field accepts dict (from StructuredError.to_dict())
Step 2: Test serialization includes last_error when set
Step 3: Test deserialization restores last_error dict
Step 4: Verify StructuredError can be reconstructed from last_error

description: Implement state migration function to move existing files to XDG

Step 2: Check for files in project directory
Step 3: Copy files to XDG workflow directory

steps: Step 1: Check if migration already done (marker file)
Step 2: Move validation-history.json to XDG if exists
Step 3: Move drift-metrics.json to XDG if exists
Step 4: Create migration marker file
Step 5: Log migration actions

description: Implement log migration from .claude-agent/logs/ to XDG logs directory
steps: Step 1: Check for .claude-agent/logs/ in project directory
Step 2: Copy log files to XDG logs directory

Step 4: Keep original files until migration verified

description: Write unit tests for state migration function
steps: Step 1: Test migration moves files correctly
Step 2: Test migration is idempotent (safe to run twice)
Step 3: Test migration preserves file contents
Step 4: Test migration handles missing files gracefully

description: Update security.py bash_security_hook to return StructuredError instead of string
steps: Step 1: Import StructuredError and error_security_block from structured_errors
Step 2: Replace string error returns with StructuredError
Step 3: Include command and reason in error context

description: Add recovery hint to security block errors suggesting config file edit

description: Ensure security block StructuredError is logged with structured error event
steps: Step 1: Call logger.log_security_block with StructuredError details

description: Update security tests to verify StructuredError return type

description: Create src/claude_agent/hooks/ directory for Claude Code hook files

description: Create hooks configuration generator for hooks.json format
steps: Step 1: Define generate_hooks_config() function
Step 2: Return dict matching Claude Code hooks.json schema
Step 3: Include SessionStart and Stop events
Step 4: Set timeout to 5000ms for both hooks

description: Implement hooks.json schema with custom timeout support
steps: Step 1: Define hook entry structure: event, script, timeout
Step 2: Support custom timeout per hook
Step 3: Validate output matches Claude Code specification

description: Create session-start.sh script template that detects active workflows
steps: Step 1: Create POSIX sh-compatible script
Step 2: Read workflow-state.json from XDG state directory
Step 3: Check if phase != 'complete'
Step 4: Output JSON with additionalContext if active workflow found
Step 5: Output empty {} if no active workflow

description: Implement context formatting in session-start.sh as markdown table
steps: Step 1: Format project path, phase, and progress as markdown table
Step 2: Include features completed count
Step 3: Include last error summary if present
Step 4: Verify markdown renders correctly

description: Add graceful handling in session-start.sh for missing state file
steps: Step 1: Check if workflow-state.json exists
Step 2: Output empty {} if file not found
Step 3: Don't error or write to stderr
Step 4: Ensure exit code is 0

description: Implement session-start.sh execution time under 5000ms timeout
steps: Step 1: Minimize file I/O operations
Step 2: Use efficient JSON parsing (jq if available, fallback to python)
Step 3: Verify script completes within timeout

description: Create session-stop.sh script template for incomplete workflow logging
steps: Step 1: Create POSIX sh-compatible script
Step 2: Read workflow-state.json from XDG state directory
Step 3: Check if phase != 'complete'
Step 4: Append warning to hooks.log if incomplete
Step 5: Always output empty {}

description: Implement hooks.log format with timestamp, level, and hook identifier
steps: Step 1: Define log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [HOOK] message
Step 2: Use ISO timestamp format
Step 3: Write to $XDG_STATE_HOME/claude-agent/logs/hooks.log
Step 4: Ensure log file is created if doesn't exist

description: Add graceful handling in session-stop.sh for missing state file
steps: Step 1: Check if workflow-state.json exists
Step 2: Skip logging if file not found
Step 3: Don't error or write to stderr
Step 4: Output empty {} regardless

description: Implement CLI command 'hooks install' to install hooks to project
steps: Step 1: Add 'hooks' command group to cli.py
Step 2: Add 'install' subcommand with project_dir argument
Step 3: Create .claude/hooks/ directory in project
Step 4: Write hooks.json, session-start.sh, session-stop.sh
Step 5: Set executable permissions on scripts

description: Set executable permission (0o755) on hook scripts during installation
steps: Step 1: Use os.chmod after writing script files
Step 2: Set permission to 0o755 (rwxr-xr-x)
Step 3: Verify scripts are executable
Step 4: Handle Windows gracefully (no chmod needed)

description: Implement CLI command 'hooks uninstall' to remove hooks from project

description: Write unit tests for phase filter in LogReader

description: Persist StructuredError to workflow state on error occurrence

description: Handle missing XDG_STATE_HOME gracefully with fallback

description: Handle permission errors when creating state directories

description: Handle missing workflow state file gracefully

description: Handle skill loading errors gracefully

description: Handle log file rotation errors gracefully

description: Handle concurrent access to state files safely
